nameMeanings.name: Bluet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for someone with fair hair or a light complexion, from Old French blund, blond (from Latin blondus) ‘blond, fair, yellow-haired’, used also of complexion.

variants.name: Blundell
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): from Old French blondel, a diminutive of blond ‘blond, fair’, used for someone with fair hair or a fair complexion and also used as a personal name. Compare Blunt .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet was born in 1440, in Holcombe Rogus, Devon, England. She had at least 1 son with Thomas Franie I.. She died about 1476, in Redlynch, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet est née en 1440, à Holcombe Rogus, Devon, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Thomas Franie I.. Elle est décédée environ 1476, à Redlynch, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet wurde 1440 in Holcombe Rogus, Devon, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Thomas Franie I. mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1476 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Redlynch, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet è nata il nel 1440 nel Holcombe Rogus, Devon, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Thomas Franie I.. Morì circa 1476, nel Redlynch, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluetは1440年にイギリスデボンホールコンベ ログスで生まれた。彼女はThomas Franie I.との間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は約1476年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトレドリンチで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet(은)는 1440년 영국 데폰 홀코브 로구스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Franie I.(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1476년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 레드린치에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet nasceu em 1440, em Holcombe Rogus, Devon, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Thomas Franie I.. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1476, em Redlynch, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet родилась в 1440 г. в местности холкомб рогус, девон, Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Thomas Franie I. Она умерла около 1476 г. в местности редлинч, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 37 лет.
